Name: Dusknoir
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw 2 cards. If you have 7 or more cards in your hand, discard a number of cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. Then, put 2 damage counters on Dusknoir. This power can’t be used if Dusknoir is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Count the number of damage counters on Dusknoir. Put that many damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Dusknoir by your opponent’s Pokémon that has 2 or less Energy attached to it during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Empoleon
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Empoleon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Active Pokémon, you may use this power. Your opponent can’t attach any Energy cards from his or her hand to his or her Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Empoleon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Infernape
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Infernape from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 4 [R] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Infernape by attacks is increased by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Infernape and this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Lumineon
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Lumineon is your Active Pokémon, you may look at your opponent’s hand. If your opponent’s Bench isn’t full, choose 1 Basic Pokémon from your opponent’s hand, and put it onto his or her Bench. Then, switch it with the Defending Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Lumineon is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Lumineon during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Magnezone
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a [L] or [M] Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Magnezone is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
Discard an Energy attached to Magnezone.

Name: Magnezone
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a [L] or [M] Energy card and attach it to your Active Pokémon. Then, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Magnezone is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may switch Magnezone with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.

Name: Mismagius
Effects: Choose up to 4 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards and Technical Machine cards in play (both yours and your opponent’s) and discard them. This attack does 20 damage times the number of cards discarded in this way.
If your opponent has 4 or more Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and return that Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your opponent’s hand.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Raichu can’t use Slice during your next turn.
Move an Energy card attached to Raichu to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Discard 3 Energy attached to any of your Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Regigigas
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have Regirock, Regice, and Registeel in play, the attack cost of Regigigas’s attacks is [C] less.
Effects: You may do 60 damage plus 40 more damage. If you do, Regigigas does 40 damage to itself.

Name: Sceptile
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), move a [G] Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Sceptile is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Remove 2 damage counters from Sceptile.

Name: Torterra
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Torterra from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may choose as many of your [G] Pokémon in play as you like. For each [G] Pokémon you choose, search your deck for an Evolution card that evolves from that Pokémon and evolve it. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Torterra does 20 damage to itself. Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.
During your opponent’s next turn, when your opponent puts a Basic Pokémon from his or her hand onto his or her Bench, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

Name: Abomasnow
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Abomasnow is your Active Pokémon, any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon, excluding [G] Pokémon and [W] Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If Abomasnow evolved from Snover during this turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Bronzong
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose a card from your hand and put it on top of your deck. Then, search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Bronzong is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Put a number of damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon equal to the number of [C] Energy in that Pokémon’s Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).

Name: Cherrim
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each of your [G] Pokémon’s and [R] Pokémon’s attacks does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

Name: Drapion
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If 2 or more of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Discard all Special Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. For each Basic Pokémon you put onto your Bench, you may search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Dusknoir
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Dusknoir is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it between turns.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon already has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The antenna on its head captures radio waves from the world of spirits that command it to take people there.

Name: Gengar
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your opponent’s turn, if Gengar would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If that Pokémon has any Poké-Powers, put 6 damage counters on that Pokémon instead.
Look at your opponent’s hand. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Trainer, Supporter, and Stadium cards in your opponent’s hand.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Does 30 damage times the number of Magikarp in your discard pile.
Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card from your opponent’s deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card from each of your opponent’s Pokémon.

Name: Machamp
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon isn’t an Evolved Pokémon, that Pokémon is Knocked Out instead of damaged by this attack.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Machamp.

Name: Mamoswine
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads or you may start again. Each time you start again, put 2 damage counters on Mamoswine. (If Mamoswine would be Knocked Out, you can’t start again.)
Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each Swinub on your Bench, plus 20 more damage for each Piloswine on your Bench, and 40 more damage for each Mamoswine on your Bench.

Name: Rapidash
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Rapidash is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Rapidash is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Rapidash and choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Roserade
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Roserade is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Roserade is Knocked Out), the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to that Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Salamence
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If your opponent has any Pokémon in play that has maximum HP of 120 or more, ignore all [C] Energy necessary to use Salamence’s attacks.
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy and a [W] Energy attached to Salamence.

Name: Scizor
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Scizor has 6 or more damage counters on it, any damage done to Scizor by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Scizor during your opponent’s next turn.
If you don’t have any Pokémon with any Poké-Powers in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Skuntank
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Skuntank from your hand to evolve 1 of your Active Pokémon, you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. If that Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, that attack does nothing.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Before doing damage, discard all Trainer cards attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Staraptor
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Staraptor is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by attacks from your opponent’s Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it on top of your opponent’s deck. Your opponent shuffles his or her deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Steelix
Effects: Choose a number of your opponent’s Pokémon up to the amount of Energy attached to Steelix. This attack does 20 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Tempered underground under high pressure and heat, its body is harder than any metal.

Name: Tangrowth
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Remove 1 damage counter from Tangrowth between turns.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If the first coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If the second coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Tyranitar
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: After your opponent’s Pokémon uses a Poké-Power, you may search your discard pile for a basic [D] Energy card and attach it to Tyranitar.
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Tyranitar.
This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Vespiquen
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, Vespiquen’s attacks do 10 more damage for each [G] Pokémon on your Bench to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: After your attack, remove from Vespiquen the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned, Paralyzed, and Poisoned.

Name: Bibarel
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, excluding damage, done to Bibarel.
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Budew
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Roselia from your hand onto Budew (this counts as evolving Budew) and remove all damage counters from Budew.
Move-Energy:
Poké-BODY ⇢ Poison Enzyme
Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Budew by your opponent’s Poisoned Pokémon.
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Dusclops
Effects: You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its body is hollow. It is said that those who look into its body are sucked into the void.

Name: Dusclops
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
If you have a Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, remove 2 damage counters from Dusclops.
Flavor-Text: Its body is hollow. It is said that those who look into its body are sucked into the void.

Name: Electrode
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Electrode is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Electrode is Knocked Out), put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: If Electrode was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Electrode
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each [L] Energy attached to Electrode. Flip a coin. If tails, discard all [L] Energy attached to Electrode.
Flavor-Text: It is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.

Name: Farfetch’d
Effects: Search your deck for a Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can’t live without the stalk it holds. That’s why it defends the stalk from attackers with its life.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

Name: Haunter
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
You may search your opponent’s discard pile for up to 3 in any combination of Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards and put them into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It can slip through any obstacle. It lurks inside walls to keep an eye on its foes.

Name: Machoke
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Machoke’s boundless power is very dangerous, so it wears a belt that suppresses its energy.

Name: Magneton
Effects: If you have a Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is actually three Magnemite linked by magnetism. A group can set off a magnetic storm.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is actually three Magnemite linked by magnetism. A group can set off a magnetic storm.

Name: Miltank
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Pichu
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Pikachu from your hand onto Pichu (this counts as evolving Pichu) and remove all damage counters from Pichu.
Effects: Search your discard pile for up to 4 [L] Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.
Flavor-Text: The electric pouches on its cheeks are still small. They cannot store much electricity yet.

Name: Piloswine
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Piloswine. Then, remove 4 damage counters from Piloswine.
Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its shaggy coat makes it unable to see. It checks surroundings with its sensitive nose instead.

Name: Pupitar
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Any damage done to Pupitar by attacks is reduced by 10 for each Energy attached to Pupitar (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can’t reduce more than 30 damage in this way.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its body is hard as bedrock. By venting pressurized gas, it can launch itself like a rocket.

Name: Sableye
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Sableye is your Active Pokémon at the beginning of the game, you go first. (If each player’s Active Pokémon has the Overeager Poké-Body, this power does nothing.)
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card and discard it. Shuffle your deck afterward. Then, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.
If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Sableye, this attack’s base damage is 40.

Name: Scyther
Effects: During your next turn, Scyther’s Slashing Strike attack’s base damage is 60.
During your next turn, Scyther can’t use Slashing Strike.
Flavor-Text: It is nearly impossible to parry its attacking scythes. Its movements are like a ninja’s.

Name: Shelgon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Within its rugged shell, its cells have begun changing. The shell peels off the instant it evolves.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Despite being clad entirely in iron-hard armor, it flies at speeds over 180 mph.

Name: Staravia
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It flies around forests and fields in search of bug Pokémon. It stays within a huge flock.

Name: Bagon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack or retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If tails, Bidoof does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Put a number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon equal to the number of [C] Energy in Bronzor’s Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

Name: Cherubi
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Cherubi.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The small ball holds the nutrients needed for evolution. Apparently, it is very sweet and tasty.

Name: Combee
Move-Energy:
Held Item ⇢ Honey
 Once during your turn, when you put Combee from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your discard pile for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench.
Effects: Draw a card. Then, you may switch Combee with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon formed by the spirits of people and Pokémon. It loves damp, humid seasons.

Name: Duskull
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on the Defending Pokémon. If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon instead.
Flavor-Text: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

Name: Duskull
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

Name: Finneon
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: After long exposure to sunlight, the patterns on its tail fins shine vividly when darkness arrives.

Name: Gastly
Effects: You opponent can’t play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent’s next turn.
You may switch Gastly with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s body is 95% made up of gases, which are blown away by strong gusts of wind.

Name: Larvitar
Effects: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that eats soil. Once it has eaten a large mountain, it goes to sleep so it can grow.

Name: Machop
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It hefts a Graveler repeatedly to strengthen its entire body. It uses every type of martial arts.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the world’s weakest Pokémon. No one knows why it has managed to survive.

Name: Magnemite
Move-Energy:
Held Item ⇢ Magnet
 Magnemite’s Retreat Cost is C less for each Magnemite on your Bench.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, Magnemite does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The units at its sides generate electromagnetic waves that keep it airborne. It feeds on electricity.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 50 damage and remove 5 damage counters from Misdreavus. If the Defending Pokémon is not Asleep, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It loves to sneak up on people late at night, then startle them with its shrieklike cry.

Name: Onix
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if Onix would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to Onix if that damage is 40 or less.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When it travels underground, it causes rumbling and tremors. It can move at 50 mph.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: If Pikachu evolved from Pichu during this turn, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Pikachu during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It lives in forests with others. It stores electricity in the pouches on its cheeks.

Name: Ponyta
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Ponyta during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: About an hour after birth, its fiery mane and tail grow out, giving it an impressive appearance.

Name: Roselia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned. If Budew is anywhere under Roselia, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Roselia raised on clean drinking water are known to grow vividly colored flowers.

Name: Skorupi
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.

Name: Snover
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Snover during your opponent’s next turn.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It lives on snowy mountains. Having had little contact with humans, it is boldly inquisitive.

Name: Starly
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.

Name: Stunky
Effects: Switch Stunky with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself by spraying a noxious fluid from its rear. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

Name: Swinub
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If tails, Swinub does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It loves eating mushrooms that grow under dead grass. It also finds hot springs while foraging.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to Tangela. Shuffle your deck afterward. If you do, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Tangela during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It is shrouded by blue vines. No one has seen the face hidden behind this growth of vines.

Name: Treecko
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: If an attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 20 more damage to that Pokémon until the end of your next turn.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on touch.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If tails, Voltorb does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on touch.

Name: Conductive Quarry
Description: Once during each player’s turn, the player may flip a coin. If heads, that player searches his or her discard pile for a [L] or [M] Energy card, shows it to the opponent, and puts it into his or her hand.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Energy Link
Description: As long as Energy Link is attached to a Pokémon, you may move an Energy card attached to that Pokémon to another of your Pokémon that has Energy Link attached to it. You may use this effect as often as you like during your turn.
Tool-Rule: Attach Energy Link to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

Name: Great Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Luxury Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon (excluding Pokémon LV.X), show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If any Luxury Ball is in your discard pile, you can’t play this card.

Name: Marley’s Request
Description: Search your discard pile for 2 different Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards, show them to your opponent, and your opponent chooses 1 of them. Put that card into your hand, and discard the other card. (If all Trainer, Supporter, and Stadium cards in your discard pile have the same name, choose 1 of them. Show that card to your opponent and put it into your hand.)
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Poké Blower +
Description: You may play 2 Poké Blower + at the same time. If you play 1 Poké Blower +, flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you play 2 Poké Blower +, choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon.

Name: Poké Drawer +
Description: You may play 2 Poké Drawer + at the same time. If you play 1 Poké Drawer +, draw a card. If you play 2 Poké Drawer +, search your deck for up to 2 cards, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Poké Healer +
Description: You may play 2 Poké Healer + at the same time. If you play 1 Poké Healer +, remove 1 damage counter and a Special Condition from 1 of your Active Pokémon. If you play 2 Poké Healer +, remove 8 damage counters and all Special Conditions from 1 of your Active Pokémon.

Name: Premier Ball
Description: Search your deck or your discard pile for a Pokémon LV.X, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you search your deck, shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Potion
Description: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if that Pokémon has only 1).

Name: Switch
Description: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Cyclone Energy
Description: Cyclone Energy provides [C] Energy. When you attach this card from your hand to your Active Pokémon, switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Benched Pokémon to switch.

Name: Warp Energy
Description: Warp Energy provides [C] Energy. When you attach this card from your hand to your Active Pokémon, switch that Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Dusknoir LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: If Dusknoir is your Active Pokémon and would be Knocked Out by damage from your opponent’s attack, you may discard all cards attached to Dusknoir LV.X and put Dusknoir LV.X as a Stadium card into play instead of discarding it. This counts as Dusknoir being Knocked Out and your opponent takes a Prize card. As long as you have Dusknoir LV.X as a Stadium card in play, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Pokémon between turns. If another Stadium card comes into play or Dusknoir LV.X is discarded by the effects of any attacks, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, Trainer, or Supporter cards, return Dusknoir LV.X to your hand.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Dusknoir. Dusknoir LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Heatran LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once at the end of your turn, if Heatran is on your Bench, you may use this power. If you discarded basic Energy cards attached to your [R] or [M] Active Pokémon by using that Pokémon’s attack this turn, attach up to 2 of those Energy cards to that Pokémon.
Move-Energy:
Poké-BODY ⇢ Heat Metal
Your opponent can’t remove the Special Condition Burned by evolving or devolving his or her Burned Pokémon. (This also includes putting a Pokémon Level-Up card onto the Burned Pokémon.) Whenever your opponent flips a coin for the Special Condition Burned between turns, treat it as tails.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Heatran. Heatran LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Machamp LV.X
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Machamp is your Active Pokémon, each of Machamp’s attacks does 60 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance) and any damage done to Machamp by your opponent’s Pokémon is increased by 60 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if Machamp would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, Machamp is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Machamp. Machamp LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Raichu LV.X
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Raichu LV.X onto Raichu and use Voltage Shoot, you may use another attack of Raichu afterward. This power can’t be used if Raichu is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard 2 [L] Energy cards from your hand and choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 80 to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Raichu. Raichu LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Regigigas LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and that Pokémon is Knocked Out. Then, search your discard pile for up to 2 basic Energy cards, attach them to Regigigas, and remove 8 damage counters from Regigigas. This power can’t be used if Regigigas is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard the top card from your opponent’s deck. Then, choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it. Regigigas can’t use Giga Blaster during your next turn.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Regigigas. Regigigas LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to Charmander.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmeleon
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to Charmeleon.
Flavor-Text: It lashes about with its tail to knock down its foe. It then tears up the fallen opponent with sharp claws.

Name: Charizard
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: All Energy attached to Charizard are [R] Energy instead of their usual type.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to Charizard.
Flavor-Text: Its wings can carry this Pokémon close to an altitude of 4,600 feet. It blows out fire at very high temperatures.

Name: Drifloon
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Drifloon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, Drifloon’s Retreat Cost is [C][C] more.
Effects: Drifloon does 50 damage to itself.

Name: Duskull
Effects: Put up to 3 damage counters on Duskull. Then, put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t attach any Energy cards from his or her hand to the Active Pokémon during his or her next turn.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Search your discard pile for a [L] Energy card and attach it to Voltorb.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
